name: Bank Of Greeleyville
description_short: Independent community bank serving South Carolina since 1935, offering checking, savings, mortgages, and online banking across three locations.
description_long:
Bank of Greeleyville is an independent community bank established in 1935, operating as a locally-owned financial institution serving Williamsburg County and surrounding areas in South Carolina. The bank operates three physical branches in Kingstree, Greeleyville, and Georgetown (opened in 2025), positioning itself as a relationship-focused alternative to larger national banks. The institution is FDIC-insured and backed by the full faith and credit of the U.S. Government, providing deposit protection up to standard insurance limits.

The bank offers a comprehensive suite of consumer and business financial products including student and adult checking accounts (including Kasasa Rewards Checking with cash rewards), savings accounts, Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) with multiple term options, real estate lending services, business checking for various business sizes, mobile and online banking platforms, debit cards (MasterMoney), overdraft protection, and financial planning calculators. Their online banking platform provides 24/7 account access, and their mobile app allows customers to manage accounts on their schedule at no charge.

Bank of Greeleyville differentiates itself through explicit emphasis on customer service reputation, no-appointment-necessary policy for account opening and loan inquiries, and direct community presence across three local branches. The bank actively markets student-focused checking products and emphasizes its long-standing tenure (nearly 90 years) serving the same geographic region, suggesting stability and local commitment rather than transactional banking relationships.

As a traditional community bank, Bank of Greeleyville is best suited for customers prioritizing personal relationships and local banking presence over digital-first experiences. The bank's geographic footprint is limited to South Carolina, making it unsuitable for customers requiring nationwide branch access. Limited information is publicly available regarding specific product rates, fees, lending requirements, or competitive positioning compared to regional and national banks. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
services: Kasasa Rewards Checking with cash rewards and no maintenance fees
Individual Retirement Accounts (IRAs) with multiple terms
pros: FDIC-insured deposits with full U.S. Government backing
Three convenient physical branch locations with extended Friday hours (until 5:30pm)
24/7 online and mobile banking access at no charge
No appointment required to open accounts or meet with loan officers
Student checking accounts with cash rewards via Kasasa partnership
Kasasa Rewards Checking with no monthly maintenance fees
Real estate lending services available for home purchase and refinancing
cons: Geographic service area limited to South Carolina (Williamsburg County and surrounding areas only)
No information provided about specific APRs, fees, or minimum balance requirements on website
Limited product comparison data or competitive rate transparency for informed decision-making
Website content appears incomplete (product descriptions cut off mid-sentence)
No mention of business loan products, lines of credit, or commercial lending services beyond basic business checking
best_for: South Carolina residents in Williamsburg County seeking local community banking with personal relationships
Students looking for no-fee checking with cash rewards and financial education focus
Homebuyers and refinancing customers in South Carolina preferring local mortgage lending
Business owners in the service area needing community bank business checking solutions
diagnosis: Bank of Greeleyville is best for South Carolina residents in or near Williamsburg County who value personalized community banking relationships and local branch access over digital convenience. The primary caveat is strict geographic limitation to South Carolina—customers requiring interstate banking or those outside the service area cannot access this bank.
typical_results_timeline: Account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions vary by product.
